refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level. Refining it into sub-FRs focused on monitoring energy use, controlling operations to reduce energy, and optimizing overall system performance aligns with the energy efficiency KPI and supports verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and comparing to E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy; confirm control actions reduce energy use; assess optimization algorithms effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy use per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system-level parameters to achieve or exceed energy efficiency targets.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and tonnage sensors with real-time telemetry.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms for energy efficiency.
DP3: Use centralized optimization service analyzing telemetry and tasking data.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between DP1 and DP3
Control commands dependency between DP3 and DP2
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking).

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable telemetry network link for data exchange.
Risk of latency impacting control effectiveness.
Assumes optimization algorithms can adapt to dynamic worksite conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure telemetry and control interfaces are robust and support real-time constraints.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level integration of energy monitoring, control, and optimization functions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchange of sensor data and status between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and operational parameters exchanged between NGHE Solution and Vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone supporting telemetry and tasking data flows.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy efficiency functions; low diffusion risk.
Contains sensors and control actuators but limited optimization logic.
Minimal direct involvement in energy efficiency functions.
No direct containment; interfaces limited to safety alerts.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing energy efficiency logic in NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports clear ownership.
containment_risks: Potential latency in telemetry data affecting control responsiveness.
Risk of overlapping functionality with Vehicle control systems.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface timing requirements and coordinate control logic boundaries with Vehicle module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FRs are well matched with selected DPs covering monitoring, control, and optimization aspects.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for control functions.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as primary owner; Vehicle module involvement is limited and well scoped.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis and energy efficiency KPI measurement at NGHE Solution level.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to NGHE Solution with well-defined interfaces to Vehicle and network.

recommended_revisions: Define strict interface timing and data quality requirements.
Coordinate control algorithm boundaries between NGHE Solution and Vehicle.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirements and architectural constraints with manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved accurately.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy use per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize system-level parameters to achieve or exceed energy efficiency targets.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Telemetry network is reliable and supports required data rates.
Optimization algorithms can adapt to dynamic conditions.
open_risks: Latency in telemetry affecting control responsiveness.
Potential overlap in control logic between NGHE Solution and Vehicle.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data latency and reliability measurements.
Validation of control algorithm performance in integrated environment.
